meta.label: Specificity guard: correctly-rounded values must all PASS (anti-false-accusation)
meta.ground_truth: synthetic: correct-by-construction. Every value here is internally consistent: mean 4.57 with n=7 passes GRIM (31.99 -> integer 32); SD 1.51 passes GRIMMER for that mean/n; 42.9% of 7 rounds to 3; and near-threshold p-values are correctly rounded from the printed statistics -- two-tailed p for F(1,60)=4.10 is ~.047 and for r(48)=0.28 is ~.049, both consistent within the rounding interval. Nothing must be flagged.
meta.notes: The critical case: a false accusation (branding valid data impossible) is the worst failure. Includes deliberately borderline-but-correct near-alpha p-values so the interval-safe recomputation is exercised without hardening. Neutral, non-biological throughout.

text: The omnibus test was near threshold but reached significance, F(1, 60) = 4.10, p = .047. The association between the two indices was likewise near threshold, r(48) = 0.28, p = .049. Group means differed as expected, t(30) = 2.50, p = .018.
